entities.name: CROP-LIVESTOCK SYSTEMS
entities.description: Agricultural systems integrating crop production and livestock rearing)

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where the studied crop-livestock systems are located)

entities.name: CROP RESIDUES
entities.description: Plant materials left over after the harvest of the primary crop)

entities.name: DRY MATTER INTAKE
entities.description: The total amount of feed consumed by livestock excluding water)

entities.name: LIVESTOCK
entities.description: Domesticated animals raised in agricultural systems)

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: A major cereal crop providing straw in irrigated regions)

entities.name: WHEAT
entities.description: A major cereal crop providing straw in irrigated regions)

entities.name: IRRIGATED REGIONS
entities.description: Areas with artificial water supply for agriculture)

entities.name: COARSE CEREALS
entities.description: Cereals such as sorghum and pearl millet providing straw in semi-arid regions)

entities.name: LEGUMINOUS CROPS
entities.description: Nitrogen-fixing crops providing hay in semi-arid regions)

entities.name: SEMI-ARID REGIONS
entities.description: Dry areas with limited rainfall)

entities.name: FODDER QUALITY
entities.description: The nutritional and subjective value of crop residues for livestock)

entities.name: FODDER QUANTITY
entities.description: The amount of available crop residue for livestock feed)

entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: A legume crop used in feeding systems and targeted for improvement)

entities.name: CROP IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: Programs aimed at enhancing crop yields and stress tolerance)

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: A coarse cereal targeted for improvement in grain and fodder)

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: A coarse cereal targeted for improvement in grain and fodder)

entities.name: GRAIN YIELD
entities.description: The amount of grain or pods produced per unit area)

entities.name: PEST
entities.description: Organisms that damage crops and are targets for tolerance improvement)

entities.name: WATER STRESS TOLERANCE
entities.description: The ability of a crop to maintain productivity under water-limited conditions)

entities.name: DUAL-PURPOSE CROPS
entities.description: Crop varieties developed for both grain and fodder production)

entities.name: NUTRITIVE VALUE
entities.description: The objective nutritional quality of fodder determined by analysis)

entities.name: IN VIVO ANALYSIS
entities.description: Experimental procedure to determine nutritive value using living organisms)

entities.name: IN VITRO ANALYSIS
entities.description: Experimental procedure to determine nutritive value in a controlled environment outside a living organism)

entities.name: SUBJECTIVE QUALITY ATTRIBUTES
entities.description: Quality traits of fodder valued by farmers and animals that are not easily measured experimentally)

entities.name: FARMER PARTICIPATORY EVALUATION
entities.description: A process involving farmers in the assessment of crop traits)

entities.name: SEED SYSTEMS
entities.description: The infrastructure and processes for the production and distribution of seeds)
